content: Indications (ASMBS/IFSO 2022): BMI ≥35 regardless of comorbidities; BMI 30-34.9 with metabolic disease (T2DM, hypertension, dyslipidemia, OSA); consider BMI 27.5-29.9 with uncontrolled T2DM. Preoperative: multidisciplinary evaluation (medical, psychological, nutritional), optimize comorbidities. Postoperative phases: clear liquids (days 1-7), full liquids (weeks 2-3), pureed (weeks 4-5), soft solids (weeks 6-8), regular diet thereafter. Lifelong supplementation mandatory: multivitamin with minerals, calcium citrate 1200-1500mg/day + vitamin D, vitamin B12 (oral/sublingual/injection), iron (especially menstruating women), additional vitamins as needed. Follow-up: frequent first year (2 weeks, 6 weeks, 3, 6, 9, 12 months), then annually with labs monitoring nutritional status. Complications: early (leak, bleeding, DVT/PE); late (strictures, ulcers, internal hernia, gallstones, nutritional deficiencies, hypoglycemia). Weight loss: sleeve ~20-25% total body weight, RYGB ~25-30%, BPD/DS ~30-35% at 1-2 years.
